title: IDRA-21
description: IDRA-21 is a benzothiadiazine derivative and a positive allosteric modulator of the glutamatergic AMPA receptor. It works by attenuating the rapid desensitization of these receptors, thereby increasing excitatory synaptic strength and promoting long-term potentiation in the brain.

research.title: The effects of IDRA 21, a positive modulator of the AMPA receptor, on delayed matching performance by young and aged rhesus monkeys
research.summary: This study found that IDRA-21 significantly improved task accuracy in monkeys for up to 48 hours after a single dose, highlighting its potential for long-lasting cognitive enhancement.

research.title: 7-Chloro-3-methyl-3,4-dihydro-2H-1,2,4-benzothiadiazine S,S-dioxide (IDRA 21), a congener of aniracetam, potently abates pharmacologically induced cognitive impairments in patas monkeys
research.summary: Researchers demonstrated that IDRA-21 effectively reverses learning deficits induced by GABA-enhancing drugs like alprazolam.

research.title: Effect of the AMPA receptor modulator IDRA 21 on LTP in hippocampal slices
research.summary: The study suggests IDRA-21 promotes long-term potentiation (LTP), the cellular basis for memory, by enhancing AMPA receptor-mediated currents.

research.title: 7-Chloro-3-methyl-3-4-dihydro-2H-1,2,4 benzothiadiazine S,S-dioxide (IDRA 21): a benzothiadiazine derivative that enhances cognition by attenuating AMPA receptor desensitization
research.summary: Research in rats showed that oral administration of IDRA-21 significantly improved performance in water maze tasks and passive avoidance tests.

research.title: The diazoxide derivative IDRA 21 enhances ischemic hippocampal neuron injury
research.summary: This critical study warns that IDRA-21 can worsen neuronal damage following a stroke or global ischemia due to excessive AMPA receptor activation.

sentiment.summary: Users generally report significant memory and focus benefits, but a high level of caution exists regarding potential excitotoxicity and long-term safety.

effects.label: Potency:
effects.description: Frequently compared to aniracetam but regarded as significantly more potent (up to 10-30 times).

effects.label: Synergy with Other Nootropics:
effects.description: Often stacked with PRL-8-53 or Racetams to maximize memory retention for exams.

effects.label: Task Difficulty:
effects.description: Research suggests it is most effective at improving performance on complex or 'difficult' cognitive tasks rather than simple ones.

effects.label: Variable Response:
effects.description: While highly effective for some, others find the stimulation 'weird' or unsettling compared to traditional stimulants.

dosage.label: Standard Oral Dose:
dosage.description: Most users report taking between 5mg and 10mg per day.

dosage.label: Frequency:
dosage.description: Due to the long half-life (noted in research as up to 48 hours), many users dose every 2-3 days rather than daily.

dosage.label: Microdosing:
dosage.description: Some users start as low as 3mg to assess sensitivity and mitigate the risk of over-excitation.

dosage.label: Intranasal Use:
dosage.description: A few users experiment with 10mg intranasaly, though there is no formal research supporting this route.

sideEffects.label: Excitotoxicity Risk:
sideEffects.description: The primary concern is permanent neuronal damage if taken during high-stress states, stroke, or seizures.

sideEffects.label: Scalp Tingling:
sideEffects.description: A common user-reported sensation described as an electric or vibrating feeling on the scalp.

sideEffects.label: Headaches:
sideEffects.description: Some users report pressure or 'brain fog' if Choline levels are not managed while using AMPA modulators.

sideEffects.label: Increased Ischemic Damage:
sideEffects.description: Scientific evidence indicates it can worsen brain damage during oxygen-deprived events like a stroke.

availability.label: Research Chemical Status:
availability.description: Primarily sold through specialized nootropic sources as a research compound rather than a dietary supplement.

availability.label: Regulatory:
availability.description: Not FDA-approved for human consumption; strictly categorized as a laboratory research chemical in many regions.
